pattern: A digit sum is divisible by three iff the sum of its parts is; greedily keep as many digits as possible by removing the fewest, highest-remainder digits to fix a non-zero remainder.
hints: The divisibility rule for 3: a number is divisible by 3 if and only if the sum of its digits is divisible by 3. Track the digit sum's remainder mod 3.
If the remainder is 0 use all digits; if it's 1, try removing one digit with remainder 1, or else two digits with remainder 2 — pick whichever keeps more digits; similarly for remainder 2.
After choosing which digits to drop, arrange the survivors in descending order to maximize the number, but guard against a leading zero (return '0' if all remaining digits are zero).

edge_inputs.description: single digit not divisible by 3 returns empty
all zeros — answer is a single zero
must remove two digits of remainder-1 to restore divisibility
leading zero in result must be avoided when non-zero digits remain

stress.description: random digit arrays up to length 10000
